descriptionShort: Langley Eagles remain three points behind Richmond Sockeyes in BCJHL standings after trading scorer Bryan Moller to the division leaders.
descriptionLong: The Langley Eagles trail the Richmond Sockeyes by three points in the BC Junior Hockey League playoff race. Despite trading top scorer Bryan Moller to the Sockeyes, Eagles coach Muzz MacPherson says the team has won three of its last four games. In the trade, Langley received defenceman Philip Von Stefenelli from Richmond. MacPherson acknowledged giving Richmond extra points through the deal but defended the move as addressing a defensive need.

attribution.headline: Langley Eagles Hot on Richmond Sockeyes Trail Despite Trading Top Marksman
